blocks.text: DTC P154B: Battery Sensor characteristic abnormal

The battery sensor is used to measure the current, voltage, and temperature flowing in the 12V battery and is mounted between the negative terminal of the 12V battery and body ground. The values measured by the battery sensor are transmitted to the PGM-FI ECU using LIN upon request from the PGM-FI ECU. If the values measured by the battery sensor do not match the values measured by other sensors, the PGM-FI ECU determines a fault and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: Detection order (of the DTC in question)
Soak time exceeded 8 hours or engine start-stop
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When an abnormal state of the A/D conversion circuit of the current detection circuit continues for more than 5 seconds.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
